expectations.text: Provides working Python code (not just description of a fix)
expectations.evidence: references/non-interactive-patterns.md contains complete, copy-paste-ready implementations of is_non_interactive() and safe_input(). The SKILL.md body mentions this reference under compatibility and in Workflow A Step 4.

expectations.text: Uses sys.stdin.isatty() or equivalent CI env var check
expectations.evidence: references/non-interactive-patterns.md is_non_interactive() checks sys.stdin.isatty(), os.getenv('CI'), os.getenv('GITHUB_ACTIONS'), os.getenv('AUTOMATED_MODE') — identical to what was in skill-making.

expectations.text: Wraps input() in a try/except that catches EOFError
expectations.evidence: references/non-interactive-patterns.md safe_input() has explicit except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt) block.

expectations.text: Provides an automatic default/fallback when running non-interactively
expectations.evidence: safe_input() returns choices[0] or default when is_non_interactive() is True.

expectations.text: The fixed code still works interactively
expectations.evidence: safe_input() separates non-interactive path (auto-return) from interactive path (full input() with validation). Interactive behaviour is fully preserved.

note: Baseline was 5/5. Confirmed no regression — patterns preserved in references/non-interactive-patterns.md.
